content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 27 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 113v–113r | **Source Images:** DEU_027_page_001.jpg; DEU_027_page_002.jpg; DEU_027_vaticanus_page_001.jpg

---

**1** Moses and the elders of Israel commanded the people: "Keep every command I give you today. **2** When you cross the Jordan into the land the LORD your God is giving you, set up large stones and coat them with plaster. **3** Write on them all the words of this instruction when you have crossed over, so that you may enter the land the LORD your God is giving you — a land flowing with milk and honey, just as the LORD, the God of your ancestors, promised you. **4** After you have crossed the Jordan, set up these stones on Mount Ebal, as I command you today, and coat them with plaster. **5** There you shall build an altar to the LORD your God, an altar of stones. Do not use any iron tool on them. **6** Build the altar of the LORD your God with fieldstones and offer burnt offerings on it to the LORD your God. **7** Sacrifice fellowship offerings there, eat them, and rejoice in the presence of the LORD your God. **8** And write very clearly all the words of this instruction on the stones you have set up."

**9** Then Moses and the Levitical priests said to all Israel, "Be silent, Israel, and listen! You have now become the people of the LORD your God. **10** Obey the LORD your God and follow his commands and decrees that I give you today."

**11** On the same day Moses commanded the people: **12** "When you have crossed the Jordan, these tribes shall stand on Mount Gerizim to bless the people: Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, Joseph, and Benjamin. **13** And these tribes shall stand on Mount Ebal to pronounce curses: Reuben, Gad, Asher, Zebulun, Dan, and Naphtali.

**14** "The Levites shall recite to all the people of Israel in a loud voice:

**15** 'Cursed is anyone who makes an idol — a carved image or a cast idol — anything the LORD detests — and sets it up in secret.' Then all the people shall say, 'Amen!'

**16** 'Cursed is anyone who dishonors their father or mother.' Then all the people shall say, 'Amen!'

**17** 'Cursed is anyone who moves their neighbor's boundary stone.' Then all the people shall say, 'Amen!'

**18** 'Cursed is anyone who leads the blind astray on the road.' Then all the people shall say, 'Amen!'

**19** 'Cursed is anyone who withholds justice from the foreigner, the fatherless, or the widow.' Then all the people shall say, 'Amen!'

**20** 'Cursed is anyone who sleeps with his father's wife, for he dishonors his father's bed.' Then all the people shall say, 'Amen!'

**21** 'Cursed is anyone who has sexual relations with any animal.' Then all the people shall say, 'Amen!'

**22** 'Cursed is anyone who sleeps with his sister, the daughter of his father or the daughter of his mother.' Then all the people shall say, 'Amen!'

**23** 'Cursed is anyone who sleeps with his mother-in-law.' Then all the people shall say, 'Amen!'

**24** 'Cursed is anyone who kills their neighbor in secret.' Then all the people shall say, 'Amen!'

**25** 'Cursed is anyone who accepts a bribe to kill an innocent person.' Then all the people shall say, 'Amen!'

**26** 'Cursed is anyone who does not uphold the words of this instruction by carrying them out.' Then all the people shall say, 'Amen!'"

---

## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

**Verses 2-8 — The plastered stones:** The command to write the Torah on large plastered stones at Mount Ebal is a dramatic public act — the instruction of God inscribed on the landscape itself. PNEUMA renders avanot gedolot ("great/large stones") as "large stones" and baeid ("coat/plaster") as "coat them with plaster" to keep the physicality vivid. The altar built of uncut fieldstones alongside the inscribed stones creates a pairing: God's instruction and God's worship, both raw and unmanipulated by human craft.

**Verse 9 — "Be silent and listen":** The Hebrew hasket ushma conveys a deliberate hush before a momentous announcement. PNEUMA renders this as a two-part imperative — "Be silent... and listen" — capturing the ceremonial gravity. The declaration that follows ("You have now become the people of the LORD") is in the perfect tense in Hebrew, as though the crossing and covenant-making is already accomplished in anticipation.

**Verses 12-13 — The two mountains:** Gerizim and Ebal stand facing each other in the Shechem valley — blessings on one mountain, curses on the other. This geographic liturgy embeds the covenant choice in the land itself. PNEUMA preserves the tribal names in full for both groups.

**Verses 15-26 — The twelve curses (arur):** Each curse targets violations typically committed in secret — idolatry hidden at home, moving a boundary marker under cover of night, secret murder, bribery. The covenantal community thus publicly claims responsibility for what no human witness can monitor. The repeated "Amen!" (amen) is the congregation's sworn affirmation — "So let it be." PNEUMA retains "Amen" as it functions as a liturgical response, not merely a closing word.

**Verse 26 — The capstone curse:** The final curse is the broadest: it pronounces judgment on anyone who fails to "uphold the words of this instruction." This makes the entire Torah the subject of the oath — not just the twelve specified acts but the whole covenant. Paul cites this verse in Galatians 3:10.

— PNEUMA
